skill.name: signup-flow-cro
skill.description: Expert guidance for optimizing signup, registration, and account creation flows to reduce friction and increase completion rates.
skill.summary: Comprehensive CRO skill for optimizing signup flows through field minimization, progressive profiling, and conversion-focused design patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill contains only documentation and guidance for signup flow optimization. All 9 static analysis findings (7 weak cryptographic algorithm, 2 system reconnaissance) are false positives caused by pattern matching on documentation text. No executable code, network calls, or system commands are present.

content.user_title: Optimize Your Signup Flow for Higher Conversions
content.value_statement: Reduce signup friction and increase registration completion rates. Get expert guidance on field optimization, progressive profiling, and conversion-focused form design.
actual_capabilities: Audit existing signup flows and identify friction points
Recommend field-by-field optimizations based on necessity
Design single-step or multi-step signup architectures
Suggest A/B test hypotheses for signup improvements
Generate complete form redesigns with copy and layout
limitations: Cannot directly implement signup changes in your codebase
Recommendations require manual implementation and testing
Does not provide analytics setup or tracking code
Cannot access your actual signup flow data or metrics

use_cases.title: Startup Founders Launching MVP
use_cases.description: Get a conversion-optimized signup flow that balances data collection with minimal friction for early users.

use_cases.title: E-commerce Store Optimization
use_cases.description: Optimize guest checkout and optional account creation to reduce cart abandonment during registration.

use_cases.title: B2B SaaS Trial Optimization
use_cases.description: Design multi-step signup flows that collect necessary segmentation data while maintaining high completion rates.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Signup Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit my signup flow and identify the top 3 friction points. Currently I have: email, password, full name, company, and phone number fields on a single page. Completion rate is around 45%.

prompt_templates.title: Field Optimization Request
prompt_templates.prompt: For each field in my B2B signup form, tell me if it should be required, optional, or deferred to later. Justify each recommendation based on conversion best practices.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Step Flow Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a 3-step signup flow for my project management tool. I need to collect: email, password, name, company, team size, use case, and role. Show the step breakdown and rationale.

prompt_templates.title: Complete Form Redesign
prompt_templates.prompt: Redesign my entire signup form. Current issues: high mobile drop-off, password errors, and users abandoning at company field. Provide complete field order, labels, placeholder text, error messages, and mobile layout.

output_examples.input: Audit my 5-field signup form with 40% completion rate
output_examples.output: Critical issues found: Phone number field causes 25% drop-off and can be deferred. Company field should be optional. Recommend: Remove phone entirely, make company optional with progressive profiling later, add social auth options. Expected impact: 15-20% completion increase.

output_examples.input: Design multi-step flow for B2B trial signup
output_examples.output: Step 1: Email only (lowest barrier). Step 2: Password + name. Step 3: Company + team size (progressive commitment). Each step has clear progress indicator. Back navigation enabled. Data persisted across refresh.

best_practices: Minimize required fields to only what is essential for initial product use
Show password requirements upfront before the user types, not after failure
Place social authentication options prominently above or beside email form
anti_patterns: Requiring email confirmation field (redundant with email validation)
Asking for phone numbers without clear justification at signup
Clearing the entire form when a single field has an error

faq.question: How many fields should my signup form have?
faq.answer: Aim for 2-3 required fields maximum: email, password, and optionally name. Every additional field reduces completion rate. Collect other data through progressive profiling after signup.

faq.question: Should I use single-step or multi-step signup?
faq.answer: Use single-step for 3 or fewer fields and simple B2C products. Use multi-step when you need 4+ fields or have complex B2B segmentation requirements. Always show progress indicators for multi-step.

faq.question: Are social login options worth adding?
faq.answer: Yes, social auth often converts 2-3x higher than email forms for B2C. For B2B, prioritize Google and Microsoft SSO. Place them prominently, often above the email form.

faq.question: Should I require email verification before product access?
faq.answer: Consider delaying verification until necessary. Let users explore the product while verification is pending. This reduces friction and allows immediate value experience.

faq.question: What is the ideal password strength requirement?
faq.answer: Show a visual strength meter updated in real-time rather than rigid rules. Allow paste functionality. Consider passwordless options like magic links for even lower friction.

faq.question: How do I optimize signup for mobile users?
faq.answer: Use 44px+ touch targets, appropriate keyboard types for each field, enable autofill, prioritize social auth to reduce typing, and use single-column layouts with sticky CTA buttons.
